isolation.type: Rust unit tests with mock transports

assertions.title: Detection validates selected agent exists before spawning transport

steps.expected: Tests pass confirming validation runs before factory call
All non-Codex agent types (PiNative, PiAcp, DroidNative, DroidAcp) return a binary name for validation
steps.observed: agent_binary_name returns Some for all 4 non-Codex types and None for Codex and GenericAcp, confirming validation executes before create_provider_over_ssh()

evidence.testOutput: running 2 tests — test result: ok. 2 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 1039 filtered out
evidence.verification: agent_binary_name() returns Some("pi") for PiNative/PiAcp and Some("droid") for DroidNative/DroidAcp; returns None for Codex/GenericAcp (no validation needed)

assertions.title: If selected agent not found, returns clear error

steps.expected: Tests pass confirming descriptive error with agent name and binary

steps.action: Code review of error message construction
steps.expected: Error message includes agent type name and binary path, SSH session cleaned up
steps.observed: Error message format: "{agent_type} agent not found on remote host — binary '{binary}' is not on PATH". Tests verify message contains agent-specific binary name (pi/droid), 'not found', and 'not on PATH'

evidence.testOutput: running 2 tests — test result: ok. 2 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 1039 filtered out
evidence.verification: For each non-Codex agent type, error message correctly references the binary name (pi for PiNative/PiAcp, droid for DroidNative/DroidAcp) and includes descriptive text about the failure

assertions.title: If selected agent found, proceeds with provider factory

steps.expected: Tests pass confirming factory is called after successful validation
All non-Codex types route through provider path; Codex and None do not
steps.observed: is_non_codex_agent returns true for PiNative, PiAcp, DroidNative, DroidAcp, GenericAcp and false for Codex and None. All binary-backed types have non-empty binary names confirming validation can succeed and factory will be reached

evidence.testOutput: running 2 tests — test result: ok. 2 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 1039 filtered out
evidence.verification: All 4 binary-backed agent types produce non-empty binary names; is_non_codex_agent correctly identifies all 5 non-Codex types for provider path routing

summary: Tested 3 assertions: all 3 passed. 6 total cargo tests executed (2 per assertion), all passed. Code review confirms validate_agent_binary_on_host is called between SSH connect and provider factory creation for all non-Codex agent types, returns descriptive error messages with agent and binary names on failure, and correctly routes to the provider factory on success.
